stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • A pulley is a simple machine consisting of a grooved wheel and a rope or belt.
  • The wheel turns about an axle while the rope runs through the groove.
  • A fixed pulley is attached to a stationary support and does not move with the load.
  • A movable pulley is attached directly to the load and moves with it.
  • In a fixed pulley, the rope changes the direction of the applied force but does not reduce the force needed to lift the load.
  • In a movable pulley, the rope is anchored at one end so that the applied force can be roughly half the weight of the load.
stem: The student wants to contrast fixed pulleys and movable pulleys. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: In a fixed pulley, the rope only changes the direction of the applied force, but in a movable pulley, the rope allows the applied force to be roughly half the weight of the load.
Fixed and movable pulleys differ in how their ropes interact with the applied force and the load.
Both fixed pulleys and movable pulleys are simple machines that consist of a grooved wheel and a rope.
In pulleys, the applied force is the force exerted on the rope, while the load is the object being lifted.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence contrasts fixed pulleys and movable pulleys, explaining that the rope in a fixed pulley only redirects the applied force, whereas in a movable pulley the rope allows the applied force to be roughly half the weight of the load.

Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence acknowledges a general difference, it doesn’t specify what the difference is. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence states a similarity rather than a contrast. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence defines two pulley-related terms; it doesn’t contrast fixed and movable pulleys.
